summary: The Hormuz-driven oil shock and February's -92,000 payroll print have collided into a stagflation threat, leaving the Fed trapped between holding against energy inflation and easing into a weakening labor market. The 10-year Treasury yield climbed to 4.19% on March 11 as a market-driven inflationary risk premium — not Fed hikes — pushed long rates higher, weighing on bond duration and equity valuations simultaneously.
